security-alert.Title: Unusual outbound data transfer from database server
security-alert.Description: db-prod-01 transferred 2.3GB to external IP 45.33.32.156 over port 443.

security-alert.Title: Suspicious data staging on web-prod-01
security-alert.Description: Large archive file created in /tmp with database dump contents.

security-alert.Title: Egress traffic to known C2 infrastructure
security-alert.Description: Outbound connection from web-prod-01 to 45.33.32.156:8443 detected.

security-alert.Title: Lateral movement detected from web-prod-01 to db-prod-01
security-alert.Description: SSH session from web-prod-01 to db-prod-01 using service account.

security-alert.Title: Anomalous DNS queries from web-prod-02
security-alert.Description: High volume of DNS TXT queries to suspicious domain from web-prod-02.

security-alert.Title: Credential harvesting tool detected on web-prod-01
security-alert.Description: Mimikatz variant detected in memory on web-prod-01.

security-alert.Title: Phishing email with credential harvesting link
security-alert.Description: User r.williams received email from spoofed sender with link to fake O365 login.

security-alert.Title: Suspicious URL click from r.williams endpoint
security-alert.Description: User r.williams clicked link to phish-domain.com from Outlook.

security-alert.Title: Suspicious login from unusual location for r.williams
security-alert.Description: Login from IP 185.220.101.34 (TOR exit node) for r.williams shortly after phishing click.

security-alert.Title: Port scan detected from 192.168.1.100
security-alert.Description: Horizontal port scan across /24 subnet detected.

security-alert.Title: Port scan detected from 192.168.1.100
security-alert.Description: Duplicate - same port scan activity continuing.

security-alert.Title: Suspicious PowerShell execution on laptop-sales-01
security-alert.Description: PowerShell process spawned with encoded command line from outlook.exe.

security-alert.Title: After-hours VPN connection from unknown device
security-alert.Description: VPN login from unregistered device MAC at 2:30 AM.

security-alert.Title: Password spray attempt detected
security-alert.Description: 50 failed logins across 10 accounts from single IP.

security-alert.Title: Ransomware indicator detected on dev-ws-03
security-alert.Description: File encryption behavior and ransom note template detected in process.

security-alert.Title: DNS request to newly registered domain
security-alert.Description: web-prod-03 resolved newly registered domain (age < 7 days).

security-alert.Title: New IAM role created in AWS production
security-alert.Description: New IAM role 'temp-admin-role' created with AdministratorAccess policy.

security-alert.Title: Unusual process tree on jenkins-01
security-alert.Description: Jenkins spawned unexpected child process curl downloading binary.
security-alert.Triage Notes: Investigating - may be legitimate CI job

security-alert.Title: Impossible travel - a.kumar logged in from US and Russia within 1h
security-alert.Description: Login from New York and Moscow IP within 45 minutes.
security-alert.Triage Notes: Verified with user - using VPN, false positive

security-alert.Title: SSL certificate mismatch on outbound connection
security-alert.Description: Connection to partner API had certificate CN mismatch.
security-alert.Triage Notes: Known issue with partner - cert renewal pending

security-alert.Title: Large file upload to cloud storage
security-alert.Description: User uploaded 500MB file to personal OneDrive from laptop-exec-01.
security-alert.Triage Notes: Verified - approved backup of presentation materials

security-alert.Title: Adware detected on laptop-sales-03
security-alert.Description: PUP.Adware.BrowserAssistant detected and quarantined.

security-alert.Title: S3 bucket policy changed to public
security-alert.Description: aws-s3-prod bucket policy modified to allow public read access.

security-alert.Title: Service account used interactively
security-alert.Description: Service account svc-db-backup used for interactive SSH login to db-prod-02.

security-alert.Title: TLS downgrade attempt on gcp-k8s-01
security-alert.Description: Connection attempted TLS 1.0 downgrade on kubernetes API.

security-alert.Title: Potentially unwanted application on dev-ws-01
security-alert.Description: Cryptocurrency mining software detected in Docker container.

security-alert.Title: Unusual cron job created on gitlab-runner-01
security-alert.Description: New cron job added by non-root user with network connection.

security-alert.Title: Bulk mailbox access by admin account
security-alert.Description: Admin account accessed 45 mailboxes in 10 minutes via EWS.

security-alert.Title: Outbound connection to sanctioned country IP
security-alert.Description: web-prod-04 established connection to IP in OFAC-sanctioned country.

security-alert.Title: USB mass storage device connected to laptop-exec-01
security-alert.Description: Unregistered USB drive plugged into executive laptop.
security-alert.Triage Notes: Verified - exec's personal backup drive, policy reminder sent

security-alert.Title: Kubernetes pod with privileged container
security-alert.Description: New pod deployed with privileged: true in gcp-k8s-01.

security-alert.Title: MFA bypass attempt detected for j.martinez
security-alert.Description: Multiple MFA push notifications sent to j.martinez in quick succession.

security-alert.Title: DHCP exhaustion warning on VLAN 20
security-alert.Description: DHCP pool utilization above 90% on developer VLAN.

security-alert.Title: Cobalt Strike beacon behavior on web-prod-04
security-alert.Description: Process behavior consistent with Cobalt Strike beacon detected.

security-alert.Title: Expired SSL certificate on internal service
security-alert.Description: Internal API endpoint serving expired certificate.

security-alert.Title: Database query spike on db-prod-02
security-alert.Description: Query volume 500% above baseline on analytics database.

security-alert.Title: Azure resource group tag modified
security-alert.Description: Tags removed from production resource group.

security-incident.Summary: Active data exfiltration from production web and database servers to external C2
security-incident.Impact: Customer PII potentially exfiltrated from primary database. 3 production servers compromised.

security-incident.Summary: Ransomware indicators detected on developer workstation dev-ws-03
security-incident.Impact: Single developer workstation encrypted. No lateral movement detected.
security-incident.Containment Notes: Workstation isolated from network. User moved to spare device. All credentials for user d.patel queued for reset.

security-incident.Summary: Phishing campaign targeting sales team - user r.williams credentials compromised
security-incident.Impact: One user account compromised. No evidence of data access beyond email.
security-incident.Containment Notes: User password reset, MFA re-enrolled. Sessions revoked. Phishing URL blocked at gateway.
security-incident.Eradication Notes: Email rule created to quarantine emails from spoofed domain. 3 additional phishing emails found and removed from other mailboxes.

security-incident.Summary: Service account svc-db-backup used for interactive login - potential compromise
security-incident.Impact: Service account with database backup privileges used interactively. Investigating scope of access.

security-incident.Summary: Adware detected and auto-quarantined on sales endpoint
security-incident.Impact: No business impact. Adware was browser extension installed by user.
security-incident.Root Cause: User installed unvetted browser extension from third-party site. Extension bundled with adware component.
security-incident.Lessons Learned: 1. Implement browser extension whitelist policy. 2. Add detection rule for PUP installations. 3. Include browser extension safety in Q2 security awareness training.
security-incident.Containment Notes: EDR auto-quarantined adware. Browser extension removed.
security-incident.Eradication Notes: Full malware scan completed. No persistence mechanisms found.
security-incident.Recovery Notes: System cleared. User educated on approved software policy.

security-incident.Summary: S3 bucket policy changed to allow public read access on production data lake
security-incident.Impact: Production S3 bucket briefly exposed to public internet. Contains analytics data.

security-incident.Summary: MFA fatigue attack on j.martinez - push bombing detected
security-incident.Impact: No confirmed unauthorized access. User reported push notifications and did not approve.
security-incident.Containment Notes: User MFA method changed from push to FIDO2. Temporary IP block on source of auth attempts.
security-incident.Eradication Notes: Password reset completed. All sessions revoked. Source IP added to blocklist.
security-incident.Recovery Notes: User re-enrolled with hardware security key. No evidence of successful access.

security-incident.Summary: Historical: Exfiltration attempt via DNS tunneling from staging environment
security-incident.Impact: Staging data exfiltrated via DNS tunneling. Staging contained synthetic test data, no real customer data.
security-incident.Root Cause: Compromised developer credentials used to access staging environment. Attacker deployed DNS tunneling tool via CI/CD pipeline misconfiguration.
security-incident.Lessons Learned: 1. Implement egress DNS filtering and monitoring. 2. Enforce separate credentials for staging vs production. 3. Add pipeline security scanning. 4. Implement network segmentation between staging and prod.
security-incident.Containment Notes: Staging environment isolated. DNS tunneling domains blocked. Compromised credentials revoked.
security-incident.Eradication Notes: CI/CD pipeline cleaned. All staging credentials rotated. DNS tunneling tool removed.
security-incident.Recovery Notes: Staging environment rebuilt from clean state. Enhanced monitoring deployed.

playbook.Name: Phishing Triage
playbook.Description: Step-by-step playbook for triaging and responding to phishing alerts. Covers email analysis, URL/attachment inspection, user notification, and credential reset.
playbook.Steps: [{"step":1,"name":"Receive Alert","description":"Review phishing alert details - sender, subject, recipients, URLs, attachments","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":2,"name":"Analyze Email Headers","description":"Examine email headers for spoofing indicators, SPF/DKIM/DMARC results","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":3,"name":"Inspect URLs/Attachments","description":"Detonate URLs in sandbox, check VirusTotal, analyze attachment in sandbox","type":"semi-automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":4,"name":"Check Click/Open Logs","description":"Query email gateway logs for who received and clicked the phishing link","type":"automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":5,"name":"Notify Affected Users","description":"Send notification to users who received the email with guidance","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":6,"name":"Block Indicators","description":"Block sender domain, phishing URL at email gateway and web proxy","type":"semi-automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":7,"name":"Reset Credentials","description":"If credentials were entered, reset passwords and revoke sessions for affected users","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":8,"name":"Remove Emails","description":"Purge phishing emails from all mailboxes using admin search-and-destroy","type":"semi-automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":9,"name":"Document Findings","description":"Update incident record with IOCs, affected users, and actions taken","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"}]

playbook.Name: Suspected Malware on Endpoint
playbook.Description: Response playbook for malware detection alerts on endpoints. Covers isolation, evidence collection, remediation, and recovery.
playbook.Steps: [{"step":1,"name":"Review Alert","description":"Examine malware detection details - file hash, process tree, network connections","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":2,"name":"Isolate Endpoint","description":"Network-isolate the endpoint via EDR console to prevent lateral movement","type":"automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":3,"name":"Collect Forensic Data","description":"Trigger EDR memory dump, process listing, autoruns, recent file changes","type":"semi-automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":4,"name":"Analyze Malware","description":"Submit sample to sandbox, check hash against threat intel feeds","type":"semi-automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":5,"name":"Check Lateral Movement","description":"Search for IOCs across other endpoints - same hash, same C2 connections","type":"automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":6,"name":"Remediate","description":"Remove malware, clean persistence mechanisms, patch exploited vulnerability","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":7,"name":"Restore Endpoint","description":"Re-image if needed, restore from backup, remove network isolation","type":"manual","role":"IT Ops"},{"step":8,"name":"Update Detections","description":"Add new IOCs to detection rules, update EDR policies","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"}]

playbook.Name: Account Compromise / MFA Reset
playbook.Description: Playbook for responding to compromised user accounts including credential reset, session revocation, and activity review.
playbook.Steps: [{"step":1,"name":"Confirm Compromise","description":"Verify account compromise through login logs, impossible travel, or user report","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":2,"name":"Disable Account","description":"Temporarily disable the compromised account in IdP","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":3,"name":"Revoke Sessions","description":"Revoke all active sessions and tokens for the account","type":"automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":4,"name":"Reset Password","description":"Force password reset with new strong password","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":5,"name":"Re-enroll MFA","description":"Remove existing MFA methods, re-enroll with verified contact method","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":6,"name":"Review Activity","description":"Audit all account activity during compromise window - emails sent/read, files accessed, config changes","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":7,"name":"Check Forwarding Rules","description":"Remove any email forwarding rules or delegated access added by attacker","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":8,"name":"Re-enable Account","description":"Re-enable account after user verifies identity via out-of-band method","type":"manual","role":"Incident Commander"},{"step":9,"name":"Monitor Account","description":"Enable enhanced logging and monitoring for 30 days post-recovery","type":"semi-automated","role":"SOC Analyst"}]

playbook.Name: Critical Vulnerability Patch Workflow
playbook.Description: Structured workflow for patching critical vulnerabilities across production assets including testing, approval, deployment, and verification.
playbook.Steps: [{"step":1,"name":"Assess Vulnerability","description":"Review CVE details, CVSS, EPSS, CISA KEV status, and affected asset criticality","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":2,"name":"Identify Affected Assets","description":"Query asset inventory for all instances of vulnerable software/version","type":"automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":3,"name":"Check Exploit Availability","description":"Search for public exploits, check if actively exploited in the wild","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":4,"name":"Obtain Patch","description":"Download patch from vendor, verify integrity with hash/signature","type":"manual","role":"Remediation Owner"},{"step":5,"name":"Test in Staging","description":"Deploy patch to staging environment, run regression tests","type":"semi-automated","role":"Remediation Owner"},{"step":6,"name":"Request Change Approval","description":"Submit emergency change request for production patching","type":"manual","role":"Remediation Owner"},{"step":7,"name":"Deploy Patch","description":"Apply patch to production systems during approved window","type":"semi-automated","role":"Remediation Owner"},{"step":8,"name":"Verify Patch","description":"Run vulnerability scan to confirm remediation, check application functionality","type":"automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":9,"name":"Close Finding","description":"Update vulnerability status to Verified/Fixed in tracking system","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"}]

playbook.Name: Data Exfiltration Investigation
playbook.Description: Investigation playbook for suspected data exfiltration events. Covers network analysis, data classification, impact assessment, and containment.
playbook.Steps: [{"step":1,"name":"Identify Data Flow","description":"Analyze network logs to identify source, destination, volume, and protocol of data transfer","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":2,"name":"Block Egress","description":"Block destination IP/domain at firewall and web proxy immediately","type":"semi-automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":3,"name":"Identify Source System","description":"Determine which internal system initiated the transfer, check for compromise indicators","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":4,"name":"Assess Data Classification","description":"Determine what data was potentially exfiltrated - PII, financial, IP, credentials","type":"manual","role":"Incident Commander"},{"step":5,"name":"Collect Evidence","description":"Preserve network captures, system logs, memory dumps for forensic analysis","type":"semi-automated","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":6,"name":"Contain Affected Systems","description":"Isolate compromised systems while preserving evidence","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":7,"name":"Assess Blast Radius","description":"Determine full scope - all systems accessed by attacker, all data potentially exposed","type":"manual","role":"Incident Commander"},{"step":8,"name":"Legal/Compliance Notification","description":"Notify legal and compliance team for breach assessment and reporting obligations","type":"manual","role":"Incident Commander"},{"step":9,"name":"Remediate Access","description":"Close attack vector, rotate credentials, patch exploited vulnerabilities","type":"manual","role":"SOC Analyst"},{"step":10,"name":"Post-Incident Report","description":"Document full timeline, impact assessment, remediation steps, and lessons learned","type":"manual","role":"Incident Commander"}]

sla-policy.Name: Incident S1 - Critical
sla-policy.Description: Critical incidents: respond in 30min, contain in 2h, resolve in 24h

sla-policy.Name: Incident S2 - High
sla-policy.Description: High severity incidents: respond in 1h, contain in 8h, resolve in 3d

sla-policy.Name: Incident S3 - Medium
sla-policy.Description: Medium incidents: respond in 4h, contain in 24h, resolve in 7d

sla-policy.Name: Incident S4 - Low
sla-policy.Description: Low severity incidents: respond in 24h, contain in 3d, resolve in 30d

sla-policy.Name: Vuln Critical - High Criticality Asset
sla-policy.Description: Critical vulns on high-criticality assets: remediate in 7 days

sla-policy.Name: Vuln Critical - Any Asset
sla-policy.Description: Critical vulns on any asset: remediate in 15 days

sla-policy.Name: Vuln High
sla-policy.Description: High severity vulns: remediate in 30 days

sla-policy.Name: Vuln Medium
sla-policy.Description: Medium severity vulns: remediate in 60 days

audit-event.Details: Auto-created S1 incident from critical data exfiltration alert
Commander j.martinez acknowledged and began investigation
Auto-created S2 incident from ransomware detection
Staging system decommission planned. Compensating controls in place.
Full post-incident review completed with lessons learned
User re-enrolled with hardware security key
